mission.title: Install and Configure MetalLB (Bare-metal Load Balancer)
mission.description: MetalLB is a load-balancer implementation for bare metal Kubernetes clusters, utilizing standard routing protocols. It is ideal for environments where cloud load balancers are not available, providing a way to expose services externally. Key trade-offs include the need for manual IP address management and potential complexity in configuration compared to cloud-native solutions.

steps.title: Add the MetalLB Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official MetalLB Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add metallb https://metallb.universe.tf

```

> This command sets up the repository for future installations.

steps.title: Install MetalLB
steps.description:
This command installs MetalLB in the 'metallb-system' namespace using the specified version.

```bash

kubectl create namespace metallb-system

helm install metallb metallb/metallb --namespace metallb-system --version 0.15.3

```

> Create the namespace for MetalLB.
> Install MetalLB with the specified version.

steps.title: Create a ConfigMap for MetalLB
steps.description:
This step configures MetalLB with a range of IP addresses to use for load balancing.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  namespace: metallb-system
  name: config
data:
  config: |
    address-pools:
    - name: default
      protocol: layer2
      addresses:
      - 192.168.1.200-192.168.1.250
EOF

```

> Replace the IP range with your own available IPs.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  namespace: metallb-system
  name: config
data:
  config: |
    address-pools:
    - name: default
      protocol: layer2
      addresses:
      - 192.168.1.200-192.168.1.250
EOF

steps.title: Verify MetalLB installation
steps.description:
Check that MetalLB components are running correctly in the cluster.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n metallb-system

kubectl get svc -n metallb-system

```

> Ensure all MetalLB pods are in the Running state.
> Verify that MetalLB services are correctly configured.

uninstall.title: Uninstall MetalLB
uninstall.description:
This step removes MetalLB from your cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources, and any services using MetalLB will lose their external IPs.

```bash

helm uninstall metallb --namespace metallb-system

kubectl delete namespace metallb-system

```

> Uninstall the MetalLB Helm release.
> Delete the MetalLB namespace and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade MetalLB
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your configuration. This step will upgrade MetalLB to a newer version while ensuring the cluster remains functional.

```bash

kubectl get configmap config -n metallb-system -o yaml > metallb-config-backup.yaml

helm upgrade metallb metallb/metallb --namespace metallb-system --version 0.15.3

kubectl get pods -n metallb-system

```

> Backup the current MetalLB configuration.
> Upgrade to the specified version.
> Verify that all MetalLB pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get configmap config -n metallb-system -o yaml > metallb-config-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'service not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service you are trying to access is not configured correctly or does not exist.

**Fix:**
Check the service configuration and ensure it is properly defined and deployed.

troubleshooting.title: MetalLB pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to misconfiguration in the ConfigMap or insufficient permissions.

**Fix:**
Review the logs of the MetalLB pods using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n metallb-system' and correct any configuration issues.

troubleshooting.title: Load balancer IP not assigned
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The IP address range defined in the ConfigMap may not be valid or available.

**Fix:**
Ensure the IP range in the ConfigMap is correct and that the addresses are not in use by other devices.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to allocate IP'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that MetalLB cannot find an available IP address in the configured range.

**Fix:**
Check the ConfigMap for the address pool and ensure there are available IPs.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for BGP peering, improved layer2 mode stability.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required when upgrading from v0.14.

resolution.summary: You should see MetalLB pods running in the 'metallb-system' namespace with the command 'kubectl get pods -n metallb-system' returning a status of 'Running'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  namespace: metallb-system
  name: config
data:
  config: |
    address-pools:
    - name: default
      protocol: layer2
      addresses:
      - 192.168.1.200-192.168.1.250
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
